<findings>

[observation] [warning] High coupling (0.63) — 19 imports target "analyze-engine"
[observation] [warning] Low cohesion (0.38) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
[observation] [warning] Coupling of 0.63 is at the warning boundary; the notion-adapter.ts and sync command both import directly from unit-analyze rather than through a dedicated sync-domain interface, increasing blast radius of analyze refactors.
[observation] [info] The adapter-registry pattern cleanly separates the dispatch mechanism from individual adapter implementations — this is a good design that will scale well as new integrations are added.
[observation] [info] src/store/index.ts acting as a barrel entrypoint mirrors the gateway pattern used elsewhere in the monorepo; ensure it re-exports only what consumers need to prevent accidental exposure of internal adapter details.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "remote-sync-adapters" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[suggestion] [info] The store zone has a 41-item facade (src/store/index.ts) providing a proper indirection layer; document this as the required import entry point for all consumers of store internals, matching the gateway convention documented in CLAUDE.md for cross-package imports.
[suggestion] [info] src/store/atomic-write.ts provides general-purpose rename-based atomic file writes but is physically co-located with PRD adapter code. Relocate to src/util/atomic-write.ts to signal its cross-concern availability and prevent it from being overlooked by non-store modules that need safe writes.
[suggestion] [warning] Zone "Remote Sync Adapters" (remote-sync-adapters) has critical risk (score: 0.63, cohesion: 0.38, coupling: 0.63) — requires refactoring before new feature development
[relationship] [warning] store → unit-analyze (19) and store → prd-graph-utilities (16) both flow in the correct direction, but because unit-analyze and prd-graph-utilities are themselves circularly coupled, the store zone inherits indirect circular exposure through its two upstream dependencies.
[relationship] [critical] unit-analyze imports 14 items back from this zone — the domain engine depending on sync adapter internals is a domain-layer contamination: persistence adapter details should not be visible to the analyze engine.
[anti-pattern] [critical] unit-analyze imports 14 items back from the store zone, meaning the domain engine holds references to persistence adapter internals (store contracts, adapter types, or notion-client details). Domain logic must not depend on persistence adapters — the correct pattern is dependency injection: the caller passes a store instance to domain functions rather than having domain functions import adapter modules directly.

</findings>

<insights>

- Low cohesion (0.38) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- High coupling (0.63) — 19 imports target "analyze-engine"
- The adapter-registry pattern with per-integration schema files (jira.ts, notion.ts) is a well-structured extensibility point — adding a new integration requires only a new schema file and registry entry.
- Coupling of 0.63 is at the warning threshold; the store imports 19 items from unit-analyze and 16 from PRD Graph Utilities, meaning sync logic is entangled with core graph operations that ideally would be abstracted.
- The integration-schemas/ subdirectory with its own index.ts barrel export is a positive architectural signal — each integration's shape is isolated and the registry is the single composition point.
- Coupling of 0.63 is at the warning boundary; the notion-adapter.ts and sync command both import directly from unit-analyze rather than through a dedicated sync-domain interface, increasing blast radius of analyze refactors.
- The adapter-registry pattern cleanly separates the dispatch mechanism from individual adapter implementations — this is a good design that will scale well as new integrations are added.
- src/store/index.ts acting as a barrel entrypoint mirrors the gateway pattern used elsewhere in the monorepo; ensure it re-exports only what consumers need to prevent accidental exposure of internal adapter details.
- Zone "remote-sync-adapters" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- The 16-import dependency from this zone into prd-graph-utilities (unit) without a significant return flow is architecturally correct — adapters may read graph utilities — but combined with the 19-import dependency into unit-analyze, the store zone has two upstream dependencies that are themselves circularly coupled, making the store zone a passive amplifier of the core circular dependency
- store → unit-analyze (19) and store → prd-graph-utilities (16) both flow in the correct direction, but because unit-analyze and prd-graph-utilities are themselves circularly coupled, the store zone inherits indirect circular exposure through its two upstream dependencies.
- unit-analyze imports 14 items back from this zone — the domain engine depending on sync adapter internals is a domain-layer contamination: persistence adapter details should not be visible to the analyze engine.
- unit-analyze imports 14 items back from the store zone, meaning the domain engine holds references to persistence adapter internals (store contracts, adapter types, or notion-client details). Domain logic must not depend on persistence adapters — the correct pattern is dependency injection: the caller passes a store instance to domain functions rather than having domain functions import adapter modules directly.
- src/store/atomic-write.ts is co-located with adapter implementations in src/store/ but provides a general-purpose safe file write utility (rename-based atomicity) with no semantic relationship to PRD adapters, sync, or schema. It is a foundation infrastructure file that would be better located in src/util/ to signal its cross-concern usability.
- store/index.ts is a 41-item facade barrel covering stores, adapters, sync, and schema systems — it provides the indirection layer that unit-analyze conspicuously lacks. The contrast explains why store coupling is more tractable despite high import counts: consumers can program against the store facade surface, while unit-analyze consumers must reach into internal module paths directly.
- notion-adapter.ts imports only from lower-tier abstractions (store contracts, notion-map.ts, notion-client.ts, schema barrel) with no cross-layer or circular dependencies, confirming that within the store zone itself, the adapter-to-contract boundary is correctly implemented — the unit-analyze reverse import issue is contained to analyze-engine, not propagated into adapter implementations.
- src/store/atomic-write.ts provides general-purpose rename-based atomic file writes but is physically co-located with PRD adapter code. Relocate to src/util/atomic-write.ts to signal its cross-concern availability and prevent it from being overlooked by non-store modules that need safe writes.
- The store zone has a 41-item facade (src/store/index.ts) providing a proper indirection layer; document this as the required import entry point for all consumers of store internals, matching the gateway convention documented in CLAUDE.md for cross-package imports.
- [call graph] 332 internal calls, 47 outgoing, 52 incoming (cohesion: 0.88, coupling: 0.12)

</insights>
